rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 22, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANTs TIMOTHY MARTEN and MARTEN CLINIC OF PLASTIC AND RECONSTRUCTIVE SURGERY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant Marten ("Defendant") has not met his initial burden of proof to show that there are no triable issues of fact. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) First, Defendant has not presented sufficient evidence to show that the statute of limitations is not subject to equitable estoppel. Defendant has not presented any evidence to controvert the allegations in the Complaint on this issue. Likewise, the separate statement does not address this issue. Second, Plaintiff's evidentiary objections to the declaration of Dr. Toth and the declaration of Nafia Weston are sustained. The supplemental declaration of Nafia Weston, filed in reply, does not cure the evidentiary objection as to the medical records; it still fails to provide a sufficient basis to authenticate these records and to establish that they are business records. See Garibay v. Hemmat, 161 Cal. App. 4th 735 (2008). Even if the Court were to consider the testimony of Dr. Toth, it does not establish that Plaintiff cannot make a prima facie claim for medical negligence, battery and negligent misrepresentation. It does not address the allegations that Plaintiff did not consent to Defendant changing the shape of her mouth, using any procedure that penetrated her face or narrowed her face. At the very least, this creates a triable issue of material fact. (See Declaration of Plaintiff filed in Opposition, 7-8.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MONICA HUJAZI, AN INDIVIDUIAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE VS. SYDNEY A. LUSCUTOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 22, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANTs SYDNEY LUSCUTOFF and LUSCUTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court treats Defendants' demurrer to the first and second causes of action brought by Monica Hujazi in her capacity as trustee as if it were a motion to strike under CCP § 436. The motion to strike is granted with 30 days leave to amend to retain a licensed attorney to represent her interests as trustee of the trust. The demurrer to the first and second causes of action filed by Hujazi as an individual are denied. The face of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") and matters judicially noticeable fail to disclose that the causes of action are time barred. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled causation and the causes of action are not uncertain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: UN PLAZA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, LP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Noticeof Motion And Moton To Compel Response To Special Interrogatories And Notice Of Request For Sanctions Of Attoreney Fees Against Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 22, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTs UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, L.P. and HILL-UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Response To Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Of Attoreney Fees Against Plaintiff. Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that from 1997 to early 2005 when she was appointed to the bench, she was a partner at Morrison & Foerster LLP, and that at some time during that period Michael Zigler was also a partner there. Judge Miller did not work with Mr. Zigler and has not maintained contact with him since he (and she) left the firm. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial, and she believes a person aware of the facts would not reasonably entertain a doubt that she would be able to be impartial. Granted. Within 10 days, Plaintiff shall provide verified further responses to Special Interrogatories. The identities of the investors and potential investors in the property are relevant. Plaintiff has not provided sufficient evidence to show that the identities of the potential investors constitute a trade secret within the meaning of Civil Code § 3426.1(d). This information is not protected by any privacy interest that would not adequately addressed by the Protective Order. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: BARBARA SMITH VS. UNITEDHEALTHCARE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 22, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT UNITEDHEALTHCARE INSURANCE COMPANY, UNITED HEALTH GROUP INC, UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH and OPTUM, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration. Granted. Defendants have shown that Plaintiff and Defendants entered into an arbitration agreement. The arbitration agreement covers the disputes at issue in the lawsuit. Defendants demanded arbitration and Plaintiff refused to arbitrate. The agreement is valid, enforceable and not unconscionable. See Dotson v. Amgen, Inc. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 975; Serpa v. California Surety Investigations, Inc. (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 695; Wilson v. United Health Group, Inc. 2012 WL 6088318 (E.D. Cal. 2012). The arbitration agreement is not substantively unconscionable on account of lack of mutuality or any other basis. Plaintiff is ordered to submit her claims to arbitration. The proceedings in this Court are stayed pending completion of arbitration or further order of the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
